steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtract every term in the second parentheses. The minus sign changes $2x^3$ to $-2x^3$ and changes $-4$ to $+4$, so both parts of the second polynomial are affected.
$$(9x^3+6)-(2x^3-4)=9x^3+6-2x^3+4$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Combine the $x^3$ terms and the constants separately to keep unlike terms from being mixed.
$$9x^3-2x^3=7x^3$$
$$6+4=10$$
$$(9x^3+6)-(2x^3-4)=7x^3+10$$
The answer is B.
